items.title: Today, we defend against hundreds and hundreds of attack drones every day, and thousands and thousands every week and every month. Ukraine’s defense sector is building that capability even in the middle of a full-scale war. We also maintain a high interception rate against cruise missiles, thanks to the way we have built our air defense – and thanks to the support of all our partners who continue to provide Ukraine with defense assistance. I have a question for you – Do you really believe it would be RIGHT to leave outside NATO a country and a people with this level of defensive capability? From a speech at the NATO Defense Industry Forum. (2/5)

We have completely eliminated the very idea of Russia having a strategic rear. For a long time, Russia believed it had a territorial advantage no one else possEssed – a deep rear where it could safely keep military production, military equipment, and everything its war depends on, believing no one could reach them. We have reached them. Just yesterday, Ukrainian drones broke-through Russia’s defenses and struck a Russian oil-refinery in Siberia. This is not an exception. It is the new reality. There is no major oil-refinery left in Russia that has not been struck by Ukraine. From a speech at the NATO Defense Industry Forum. (3/5)

From a speech at the NATO Defense Industry Forum. (1/5)

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: I met with the @SecGenNATO in Ankara. I thank Mark Rutte for his personal support and for helping Ukraine and our people. I briefed Mark on the consequences of Russia's attacks on Ukrainian cities and communities. We discussed the protection of our people from Russian ballistic threats and the need for urgent deliveries of missiles for air defense systems. A separate part of our discussion focused on additional contributions from Allied countries to the PURL initiative and efforts to establish an Anti-Ballistic Coalition. We also covered joint weapons production, as well as strengthening defense cooperation between Ukraine and NATO countries. Right now, it is critically important that every channel supporting our air defense – both bilateral and multilateral – operate at full capacity. Mark commended the achievements of our warriors on the front line, who continue to inflict heavy losses on Russia, as well as the results of our long-range responses. We agreed to work swiftly together to implement everything we discussed during the meeting.

items.title: R to @ZelenskyyUa: Зустрівся з Генеральним секретарем НАТО в Анкарі. Дякую Марку Рютте за особисту підтримку та допомогу Україні й нашому народу. Поінформував Марка про наслідки російських атак на українські міста й громади. Обговорили захист наших людей від російської балістики та необхідність термінових поставок ракет до систем ППО. Окремо говорили про додаткові внески країн Альянсу у програму PURL та зусилля для створення антибалістичної коаліції. Обговорили і спільне виробництво зброї та посилення оборонної співпраці між Україною та країнами НАТО. Зараз дуже важливо, щоб усі канали підтримки нашого захисту неба – як двосторонні, так і багатосторонні – працювали максимально активно. Марк відзначив успіхи наших воїнів на фронті, які завдають Росії великих втрат, а також результати наших далекобійних відповідей. Домовились оперативно співпрацювати, щоб реалізувати все, про що говорили під час зустрічі.
items.summary:
Зустрівся з Генеральним секретарем НАТО в Анкарі. Дякую Марку Рютте за особисту підтримку та допомогу Україні й нашому народу.

items.title: I believe Europe urgently needs its own capability to produce anti-ballistic systems and the missiles they require. We all value the Patriot system. It is an excellent system. There are others as well. But today’s wars have shown that current Patriot production is not enough to meet the growing demand for protection against ballistic missiles.That is a fact. And we must respond to that fact. Those who defend life need more Patriots. We have also discussed Patriot production licenses with our American partners. I ask you to support our efforts to make this happen. From a speech at the NATO Defense Industry Forum. (5/5)

items.title: BP Drove Up European Jet Fuel Output by 30% During Iran Conflict
items.summary: BP Plc boosted jet fuel production at its European refineries by about 30% during the Iran conflict, helping the region avoid major shortages while millions of barrels were locked up in the Persian Gulf.
